response.justification: Bogdan has a credible long-form engineering career with genuine high-throughput distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic), a current Staff Engineer title, and hands-on AI-agent and MCP tooling work that aligns directly with the AI-native environment described in the JD. However, Python — listed as a must-have — is entirely absent from the resume and skills list, which is a material disqualifier unless addressed. The most substantive distributed systems work is now 6+ years old, recent experience skews toward product full-stack, and there is limited evidence of cross-functional technical strategy ownership or stakeholder coordination at the scale expected of a Staff-level IC.
strengths: Current role includes hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, directly matching the AI-native engineering baseline the JD treats as a given
Documented high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and low-latency design
Consistent Staff-level IC track record across tooling, open-source platforms, and cross-stack ownership spanning 15+ years with multiple staff title appointments
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume and skills inventory despite being listed as a must-have technical skill in the JD
The most substantive high-throughput distributed systems work (Sociomantic, 2018–2020) is now over six years old; recent work is predominantly product full-stack rather than architecture at scale
Evidence of technical strategy ownership, cross-functional stakeholder management, and driving engineering decisions at organizational scope is thin relative to Staff-level expectations
